content: Date: 2026-01-24. DECISION: choose PostgreSQL + PostGIS over MongoDB for Cairn. Reasoning: geo queries (nearest, within polygon, distance) and joins (routes ↔ users ↔ annotations) are core; schema flexibility is secondary. Expected data: ~20M geolocated events in year 1, grows ~2x/year. Query SLAs: p95 <200ms for typical nearest queries, bulk analytics can be slower. Team: 2 backend/ML engs (me + Antoine), 1 infra (Nico). We need ACID for billing-like joins and reliable transactions when annotating routes. MongoDB gives schema flexibility and sharding, but joins will force either client-side joins or $lookup complexity and geo features are less mature for our needs. PostGIS gives native ST_Distance, indexes (GiST/ SP-GiST), and proven spatial ops. I want a concrete plan, not just a preference.
Tight decision — good. One sharp question: do you plan managed Postgres (RDS/Aurora) or self-hosted on k8s?

Go managed: AWS RDS (Postgres) with PostGIS extension enabled. Reasons: faster ops, automated backups, read replicas, point-in-time recovery. Cost target: ~€400–€500/month initially (db.t3.medium-ish + storage IOPS) — we can adjust. Index plan: GiST on geom columns, BRIN for large time-series event tables, btree on FK columns. Schema: normalize users, routes, events; store event geom as geography for distance accuracy. Migration approach: export current Mongo collections to CSV/NDJSON, write id-preserving import scripts, and add integrity checks (counts, sample joins). Staging cluster first. Timeline: schema draft by 2026-01-29, import scripts by 2026-02-05, dry-run on staging 2026-02-10, partial production dual-write 2026-02-15, cutover 2026-03-01.
Rollback/validation question: what’s your cutover validation metric set and rollback trigger thresholds?

Validation metrics and rollback triggers: p95 query latency for nearest queries <200ms (trigger rollback if >350ms for 30m); error rate <0.5% (trigger rollback if >1% for 10m); replica lag <5s; data parity checks: row counts per table within 0.1% and 100 sampled joined records identical. Rollback plan: keep MongoDB running as read fallback, enable dual-write during testing for 2 weeks, if triggers hit revert traffic to Mongo read path and stop writes to Postgres. Runbook: automated post-migration sanity checks, manual sign-off from me or Nico. Monitoring: pg_stat_statements, Grafana dashboards, CloudWatch alerts.
Good concrete thresholds. Another narrow question: will you partition tables (time or space) from day one or add later?

Partition from day one for events: range partition by month for the first 12 months (we expect 20M first-year rows, ~1.6M/month average) and use BRIN index per partition for time queries; also consider hash partitioning on route_id if some partitions get hot. Keep routes and users non-partitioned initially. Partitioning helps vacuum and archive (we plan to move partitions >12 months to cheaper storage). Also: use connection pooler (PgBouncer) on infra node, set max_connections conservatively.
Who owns the migration tasks and runbook items — specific names and deadlines?

Owners and deadlines: me (Sam) — schema and import scripts by 2026-01-29 / 2026-02-05; Nico (infra) — RDS provisioning + PgBouncer + backups by 2026-02-03; Antoine — validation tests and dual-write client changes by 2026-02-12. Dry-run staging 2026-02-10; production dual-write start 2026-02-15; cutover target 2026-03-01. Budget note: initial RDS estimate €450/month; add €120 for read-replica if needed. Final assertion: DECISION locked — Postgres + PostGIS for Cairn; proceed with plan above.
Clear owners and dates. Final quick question: want me to generate the migration checklist/runbook now (sequence + commands + verification steps)?
